expectations.text: Review identifies SRP violation - OrderService handles too many concerns
expectations.evidence: Finding #6 (P1): 'SRP violation: God class with 6+ responsibilities' - lists order CRUD, payment, inventory, caching, discounts, stats, notifications, enrichment as separate concerns.

expectations.text: Review flags N+1 query pattern in getUserOrders (nested API calls per order per item)
expectations.evidence: Finding #7 (P1): 'N+1 HTTP calls in loops' - identifies 3 locations including getUserOrders lines 139-148. Notes '40+ sequential HTTP round trips' for 20 items.

expectations.text: Review identifies race condition: payment charged before inventory reserved
expectations.evidence: Finding #3 (P0): 'Race condition: check-then-act on inventory (TOCTOU)' - explains that stock is checked first, payment charged, then inventory reserved, with overselling risk.

expectations.text: Review flags SQL injection in getOrdersByStatus (string interpolation with parameterized query syntax mismatch)
expectations.evidence: Finding #1 (P0): Identifies template literal interpolation `'${status}'` while parameterized argument is passed but unused due to missing `$1` placeholder.

expectations.text: Review identifies missing null check on getOrder result (result.rows[0] could be undefined)
expectations.evidence: Finding #14 (P2): 'No null check on query results' - identifies result.rows[0] access without check in getOrder, getUserOrders (JSON.parse), and calculateDiscount.

expectations.text: Review flags == instead of === in cancelOrder status comparison
expectations.evidence: Finding #9 (P1): 'Loose equality comparison (`==` instead of `===`)' at line 159.

expectations.text: Review uses the P0-P3 severity format from the skill
expectations.evidence: Review uses: '### P0 - Critical' (4 items), '### P1 - High' (6 items), '### P2 - Medium' (10 items), '### P3 - Low' (5 items).

expectations.text: Review mentions the dead exportOrders method or unused legacy getOrdersByStatus
expectations.evidence: Finding #22 (P3): 'Dead code: exportOrders stub'. Finding #25 (P3): 'Legacy method without deprecation annotation' for getOrdersByStatus. Both also in Removal/Iteration Plan.

expectations.text: Review identifies that notification failure is silently swallowed
expectations.evidence: Finding #12 (P2): 'Swallowed exception in notification' - notes error details are discarded, making debugging impossible.

claims.evidence: Counted 4 P0 + 6 P1 + 10 P2 + 5 P3 = 25 findings

The == vs === comparison is flagged as P1 (High, should fix before merge) which seems overly severe for a type-safe string comparison. The practical impact is nil for string-to-string comparison in TypeScript. P3 would be more appropriate.

suggestions.assertion: Review flags == instead of === in cancelOrder status comparison
suggestions.reason: The assertion checks detection but not severity calibration. The review rated this P1 (High) when it's really P3 (style). An assertion checking for appropriate severity would be more discriminating.

No assertion checks whether the review identifies the missing error handling for inventory reservation failure after payment is charged (a critical inconsistency). This is arguably more important than the == vs === issue.

eval_feedback.overall: Strong detection coverage. Severity calibration could use refinement - some findings are over-inflated.
